tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: I have a free afternoon and a high-priority scholarship deadline approaching, but the award is substantial enough to justify prioritizing it over immediate fun.
Early progress on the scholarship application creates a buffer against potential delays, making this an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a high-stakes crisis.
After one hour, the scholarship application is only 32% complete with three days remaining. The initial progress created a buffer, but half my free time is now gone without finishing the task.
I made tangible progress on the scholarship application, reducing the remaining gap to completion. However, I missed a casual gaming opportunity that is no longer available.
After one hour of work, the scholarship application is 55% complete with three days left. While progress is steady, I still need to double my daily output to finish on time.
Two hours of work have pushed the scholarship application to 64% completion, though I still need roughly one more focused hour to finish before the deadline.

After working an additional hour, my scholarship application is now 68% complete with three days remaining. I am closer to finishing the required essay and uploads but still need about two more focused hours.
After 1.5 hours, the scholarship application is 74% complete with three days left; I have roughly 2.3 hours of work remaining to finish before the deadline.
I slept 6 hours with no debt, maintaining high energy and low stress. This restful state positions me well to complete the remaining scholarship work efficiently.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after two hours of focused work, leaving me with no remaining tasks for this deadline.
The scholarship application is now fully complete after a final 0.5-hour check, confirming the task is done with three days to spare.
I let the moment pass at 08:30 without acting, despite having completed all scholarship work hours ago.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
